intro: Ocimum dolomiticola, also known as Dolomitic basil, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the high-altitude regions of Ethiopia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Ocimum dolomiticola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Dolomitic basil is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which are used to treat respiratory and digestive issues.
